title: 2022 Hull Kingston Rovers season
wikitext:
{{Short description|English rugby league season}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | year = 2022 | team_colour = #b50000 | font_colour = white; | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XXVII|Super League]] | league_rank = 8th | league_wins = | league_draws = | league_losses = | points_for = | points_against = | playoff_result = | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Semi-finals | cup_wins = | cup_losses = | CEO_title = | CEO = Neil Hudgell | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]]
{{nowrap|(until 4 July 2022)}}
[[Danny McGuire]]
{{nowrap|(from 4 July 2022)}} | captain = | stadium = [[Craven Park, Hull|KCOM Craven Park]]
[[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]] | agg_attendance = | avg_attendance = | high_attendance = | low_attendance = | top_tries = | top_goals = | top_points = | prev_season = [[2021 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2023]] | list = no | list_link = List of Hull Kingston Rovers seasons }} In the 2022 rugby league season, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] competed in [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. They were coached by [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] until 4 July 2022 and [[Danny McGuire]] from 4 July 2022 onwards. ==Season review== On 11 February, Hull Kingston Rovers began their Super League campaign with a loss to [[Wigan Warriors]], followed a week later by a defeat at [[Huddersfield Giants]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} The first win came in Round 3 at home to [[Castleford Tigers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36688/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League round-up|publisher=RFL |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} The most successful period of the season came in April with a 34–10 win over Castleford in the quarter-final of the [[Challenge Cup]] and a run of four consecutive league wins that placed them in the top half of the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37006/betfred-challenge-cup-quarter-final-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup quarter-final round-up |publisher=RFL |date=10 April 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37209/betfred-challenge-cup-preview-huddersfield-giants-v-hull-kr |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Preview: Huddersfield Giants v Hull KR |publisher=RFL |date=5 May 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} In May, the Challenge Cup campaign came to an end with a 25–4 loss to Huddersfield in the semi-finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37227/huddersfield-sweep-aside-hull-kr-to-secure-first-final-appearance-since-2009 |title=Huddersfield sweep aside Hull KR to secure first Final appearance since 2009 |publisher=RFL |date=7 May 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} A poor mid-season included a run of three league losses{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60197/betfred-super-league-round-14-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 14 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=5 June 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} followed by a further run of four defeats culminating with a 34–28 loss to [[Hull F.C.]] at the [[Magic Weekend]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60469/betfred-super-league-round-18-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 18 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=11 July 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Hopes of a play-off place were kept alive in July with back-to-back wins over [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Warrington Wolves]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60519/betfred-super-league-round-19-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 19 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=17 July 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60566/betfred-super-league-round-20-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 20 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=24 July 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} but a number of losses followed so that, even with a 38–28 win over Wigan in the penultimate round, Hull KR could no longer qualify and their season ended with a 36–4 away over local rivals Hull F.C.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60825/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=30 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} ==Results== {{clear}} ===Pre-season friendlies=== {{legend2|#CFC|Win|border=1px solid #000000|text=W}} {{legend2|#FFE3E3|Loss|border=1px solid #000000|text=L}}
